title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body:
I leave for basic in 6 days, although I'm pretty nervous I can't wait to experience it & become a better me.
(Update 1): Finally graduated BCT a few days ago, I am now in Arizona for AIT.

(Update 2): Done with AIT. Graduated damn near top of my class. Off to Airborne School.

(Update 3): Finished airborne a while ago, now to wait out my contract lol. It’s all relatively easy in retrospect, just do the right shit at the right time in the right uniform.

(Update 4): I have maybe like, 18 months until the end of my contract? Roughly? Time flies.

(Update 5:) One year left, in the TAP (Transition Assistance Program) process as of current. (or so I thought)

(Update 6:) Forgot about this comment for a while, but I just reenlisted for 4 in Hawaii. Probably gonna stay in for a few contracts tbh. (This was June of 2025 roughly, I think.)

(Update 7, since a lot of you wanted some more. Guess I gotta keep this going as a little journal for you guys or something) Currently February 11th, 2026, I shipped out for basic on January 24th, 2023. I'm on my second contract in Hawaii now, I love it here aside from all the traffic. Told myself and everyone else I wasn't gonna do a second but here I am. Have my SGT promotion board next week, got about a week and a half notice about it so I've been studying my ass off. I'll probably forget about this comment for awhile, so, see you guys next update.
author: @SampsonWrestling

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: I went to basic in 1979, scared to death. I was in the last OSUT unit at Ft Gordon. I went from being a civilian  executive secretary to the Distinguished Honor graduate! I proudly wear my veteran t shirts. Best part: when I came home and saw my dad's face! And then watching him trying to pick up his 5'3" soldier daughter's full duffle bag!!❤. Proud to be a US Army Veteran! ❤️🇺🇸 Keep it strong!

author: @boalexander3918

title: Missouri State University Virtual Campus Tour
body: Thank you Rachel.  I am an '83 alumnus of then named Southwest Missouri State, and I have not even been to Springfield since my graduation.  I am a native of California and live there again, so MS is not an easy commute for me.  I fully realize that your vlog was geared towards potential students, but you also managed to make a dinosaur happy.  The campus has changed significantly since my days there.  Cheek used to be for computers, Siceluff for business, both of which are now in Glass, which did not exist back in my day.  I use to live in a rental house on S. Kimbrough and I don't even know if that house still exists (it was 2 doors south of the Kappa Alpha house back then, and for me, that was a mistake.  They stole my bike, and even admitted it.).  The food courts you included in your video were not even figments of someone's imagination yet.  The football stadium is a newer version, and I can't say I recognized anything beyond the image of Carrington.  I do appreciated being able to take a glimpse of the old campus, even though it is not as old as I expected.  We did have a fountain back then, but I don't think it is the same as what you have now.  That promenade area, in front of the football field and fountain area use to have a name, but I can't remember it now.  Graduation was held in the basketball stadium (where the games are played) and there was once a concert there featuring the Oak Ridge Boys.  A small hint, the mall did not exist.  Downtown was a small area north / northwest of campus, and a second business area was around the hospital.  I can't remember much more.  Thanks for sharing.
author: @BDTrooper76

SINCE THE GETTING OF ADMISSION WITH NEU , THE F-1 VISAS ARE NOT YET RELEASED
author: @kotasreenivas5412

title: College tour in USA ||Northwest Missouri State University||telugu students #usarao
body: Good Morning Brother.  At the out set I  honestly incline to convey you a very very happy new year and Sankranthi.   Finally we deferred  my admission and sent the request to the connected North Eastern University Authorities.  Can you please inform me that in howmany days I will get the fresh I-20, so as to enable me to join in the very next intake?

title: College tour in USA ||Northwest Missouri State University||telugu students #usarao
body: నమస్తే సార్ ,  గతంలో కూడా మీరు నేను అడిగిన doubts కు చాలా బాగా స్పందించారు .  చాలా చాలా ధన్యవాదములు .   USA లో  MS IN REGULATORY AFFAIRS కోర్సు చదవడానికి ఏమైనా  BUDGET FRIENDLY UNIVERSITIES ఉంటే వాటి లిస్ట్ పంపగలరు . ఈ  ఒక్క  సహాయం తప్పక చేయగలరని  విశ్వసిస్తున్నాను .   ఆ యూనివర్సిటీ  duolingo  ఇంగ్షీషు టెస్ట్ ను అనుమతించాలి .
author: @kotasreenivas5412
